name: Houthis (Yemen)
summary: Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **RHETORIC ESCALATED, ACTION STILL ABSENT — AND THE HAIFA-SHIPPING TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED TODAY (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT). The escalation rhetoric now explicitly bundles Bab el-Mandeb into the threat set — Iran/Tasnim vowed the 'resistance front' would 'activate the Bab al-Mandab,' and the IRGC Quds Force commander said Israeli operations in Lebanon/Gaza would lead the Axis to create a similar 'traffic situation' in BOTH Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz (CONFIRMED that the threat was MADE; the closure itself is unimplemented). But the ACTION remains absent: no confirmed new Houthi/Axis attack on neutral commercial shipping surfaced this window — coverage stays in forecast/threat framing, and the circulating 'confirmed attack' items trace to EARLIER dates (the July Magic Seas incident is later/separate; the 'Houthis announce end of attacks' headline is the May 2025 Oman-mediated pause — a stale-source trap, avoided). **TRIGGER DECISION (June 2)**: the 'Confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days of the 19 May maritime-blockade announcement (by 2026-06-02)' trigger reaches its DEADLINE TODAY with NO confirmed qualifying attack — moved `watching` -> `resolved` (EXPIRED UNTRIPPED), a confirmed non-event despite the loud rhetoric. No edge weight change. The double-chokepoint wildcard stays at watching, now with elevated rhetorical pressure but no action. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **RED SEA THREAT-ACTIVE, NOT ACTION-ACTIVE — NO CONFIRMED LATE-MAY VESSEL ATTACK (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT). Searches surfaced threats and 'signals' of renewed Houthi Red Sea attacks (gCaptain: 'Red Sea Corridor Slips Back Into Crisis as Houthi Threats Resurface') but NO confirmed new merchant-vessel strike in late May 2026. The widely-circulated 'Scarlet Ray' tanker attack traces to September 2025, not now — a stale-source trap avoided. The double-chokepoint wildcard stays at watching. RHETORIC/THREAT only — no weight change.** Day 95 (Wednesday May 27; ~48h coverage, no brief May 26). **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' FALSIFIED — THE LLOYD'S JWC EASTERN-MED/HAIFA 7-DAY TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED MAY 26 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT).** No confirmed JWC listing (Listed Areas still at JWLA-033, March 3, 2026) and no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel reached the May 26 deadline, so the Lloyd's-JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa trigger moves `watching` → resolved (expired untripped, 'no JWC listing'). Analysts (Maritime Executive) note the Houthis lack the naval reach to enforce a Mediterranean blockade directly and can only pressure shipowners who choose to call at Haifa — the cleanest external falsification of the May 19 blockade rhetoric. The separate Houthi-kinetic-on-Haifa-bound-shipping trigger stays `watching` (CONFIRMED non-event through May 27, ~Day 8 of 14, by 2026-06-02). The ~48h window's kinetic escalation is on the IRAN axis (US self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site) and the LEBANON front (expanded Israeli ground op) — NEITHER is a Houthi/Red-Sea action; the Houthi axis remains rhetoric-without-action. A CONFIRMED non-event generates no edge weight changes. PRIOR (May 25): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 7 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED HOUTHI KINETIC ATTACK ON A HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL THROUGH MAY 25 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT). LLOYD'S JWC LISTED AREAS REMAIN AT JWLA-033 (MARCH 3, 2026) WITH NO EASTERN MED / HAIFA ADDITION — DAY 7 OF 7, ON TRACK TO EXPIRE UNTRIPPED TOMORROW (MAY 26).** Search confirms the Houthis 'have not resumed attacks since the start of the current confrontation' despite retaining capability; the Haifa blockade remains a remote/declaratory measure announced May 19. The insurance market's refusal to list the Eastern Med remains the cleanest external falsification of the Haifa-blockade rhetoric. The Lloyd's-JWC-Eastern-Med trigger stays `watching` Day 7 of 7 — it EXPIRES TOMORROW (May 26), not today, so it is NOT marked tripped; on track to close UNTRIPPED. The Haifa-kinetic-attack trigger remains `watching` Day 7 of 14 (by 2026-06-02). Note the broader regional kinetic environment has intensified on the LEBANON front (Hezbollah 1,000+ drones / 700+ rockets over 8 days, US weighing a greenlight for an expanded Israeli operation) — but that is a Lebanon-axis development, NOT a Houthi/Red-Sea action; the Houthi axis remains rhetoric-without-action. PRIOR (May 24): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 6 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED HOUTHI KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND COMMERCIAL VESSEL THROUGH MAY 24. LLOYD'S JWC DAY 6 OF 7 WITH NO EASTERN MED LISTING CHANGE — JWLA-033 (MARCH 3) STILL OPERATIVE. AWRP ~1% HOLDING WITH NO PREMIUM SPIKE ON HAIFA-BLOCKADE RHETORIC. UKMTO ADVISORIES LIST 41 INCIDENTS SINCE FEB 28; NO NEW MAY 23-24 INCIDENT MATCHING HAIFA-BOUND CRITERIA. THE INSURANCE MARKET CONTINUES TO REFUSE THE HOUTHI NARRATIVE — THIS IS THE CLEANEST EXTERNAL FALSIFICATION OF THE THREAT.** Six days after Saree announcement, no confirmed kinetic attack. The Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger (Day 6 of 7, by 2026-05-26) is on Tuesday's wire; the Haifa-kinetic-attack trigger (Day 6 of 14, by 2026-06-02) remains `watching`. **The Houthi proxy network continues to demonstrate graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — consistent with the broader Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric coexists with deal architecture (May 24 strike-window expired without kinetic action; Iran's Tasnim military-source piece NOT followed up Sunday). PRIOR (May 23): HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 5 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL CONFIRMED MAY 20-23 IN MAJOR MARITIME TRACKING OUTLETS. LLOYD'S JWC DAY 5 OF 7 WITH NO EASTERN MED LISTING CHANGE — INSURANCE MARKET NOT BUYING THE THREAT.** Five days after Saree announcement, no major maritime tracking outlet (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, The Loadstar, Supply Chain Brain) has reported a confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel. Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) remains current published listing — no Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa addition through May 23. **The cleanest external test of credibility — the insurance market — has not endorsed the Houthi threat.** Atlantic Council framing holds to Day 5: 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets.' The Loadstar May 22-23 introduces a new tangent — Houthis may target 'Egypt's Red Sea ports' — but remains rhetoric only without operational confirmation. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger Day 5 of 7 (by 2026-05-26) remains `watching`; Haifa-kinetic-attack trigger Day 5 of 14 (by 2026-06-02) remains `watching`.** **The Houthi proxy network continues to demonstrate graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — the same Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric coexists with deal architecture. Times of Israel + Haaretz May 22-23 carry no Haifa-attack reporting; AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 (Argus, Lloyd's List wire); UKMTO has not issued fresh advisory. PRIOR (May 22): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS, NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL, LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after the May 19 announcement (Houthi spokesman Saree, Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic), no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain — all running the May 17-19 announcement story; no kinetic follow-through reported May 20-22). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to the Persian Gulf coverage area — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa (Insurance Journal, LMA Joint War Committee, Lloyd's List). **The 'Lloyd's JWC adds Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to high-risk listing within 7 days (by 2026-05-26)' trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7** — the cleanest external test of credibility still uncrossed. The 'confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days' trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 14. **Atlantic Council framing — 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets' — is validated to Day 4**: rhetoric WITHOUT operational follow-through is the dominant pattern, not capability-test failure. **Israeli media confirms negative space**: Times of Israel May 22 liveblog and Haaretz carry no Haifa-attack incident reporting for May 20-22; insurance market AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels (Argus, Lloyd's List). **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. PRIOR (May 19): **A6 HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF ISRAEL'S HAIFA PORT — CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION; REVERSES MONDAY'S 'COORDINATED QUIETISM' READING.** Spokesman Yahya Saree said in a televised statement that 'Haifa has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets' and warned all companies with ships present in or heading to Haifa that the port had been 'included in the list of targets' (Reuters wire primary via Yahoo/gCaptain/Ship&Bunker; Maritime Executive; Anadolu Agency). The Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added that a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply, with all vessels 'prohibited' from loading or unloading at Israel's largest seaport. **The announcement IS rhetorically calibrated**: (i) targets Israel directly, not US/UK shipping; (ii) Haifa is on the Israeli MEDITERRANEAN coast, not Red Sea — 'long-distance naval blockade' of a Mediterranean port from Yemen is more rhetorical than operational; (iii) the announcement is justified by Gaza, NOT by Iran-US kinetic preparations — preserving the Houthis' option to claim they haven't entered the Iran-US theater. **The Maritime-Executive headline uses scare quotes around 'blockade' precisely because it has NOT been operationalized through confirmed attacks on shipping at Haifa.** Saree cited the Eilat-port blockade as precedent (REPORTED — Houthi claim that Eilat 'has since ceased operations'; independent maritime confirmation pending). **B4 The Eilat-blockade precedent is structurally weak**: Eilat is Red Sea-adjacent to Yemen's launch zones; Haifa is on the Mediterranean, far from Houthi missile/drone reach. The credibility-projection is rhetorical, not operational. **Atlantic Council / Stimson framing**: 'targeted provocations highlight the fundamental problem with partial regional ceasefires — they merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' The 'coordinated quietism' framing (Monday May 18 Day 7 UNTRIPPED) was a graduated-rhetoric pattern, NOT a structural restraint. **Iran's proxy network can pivot from non-attack (last 7 days) to verbal-blockade (today) within 48 hours.** **NEW trigger watch**: confirmed missile/drone aimed at Haifa-bound shipping within 7 days = CONFIRMED ACTION upgrade; silence by Day 7 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be the cleanest operational test. PRIOR (May 18): **A4 RED SEA NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase** (NYT preparation May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17) **is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern** when Houthi attacks compounded with US escalation rhetoric. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation. **PRIOR (May 17): B4 HOUTHIS RED SEA — NO CLAIMED OR UNCLAIMED MARITIME INCIDENT THROUGH MAY 17 MORNING IST. Negative-space holds Day 6 of 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah.** No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST. The May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident still stands as a single event — no second Red Sea attack within the 7-day window opened. Project Freedom pause Day 12; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not a coincidence.** If no incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. **PRIOR (May 14):** Day 82. A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE: Day 3 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed explosion — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). If no second incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61 (no fresh redirect actions). The Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains the operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. PRIOR (May 13): UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km (80 mi) southwest of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; the ship continued voyage with no injuries, and the vessel was briefly 'not under command' (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO advisory primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim responsibility — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** The non-claim is itself a signal: possibilities include (i) deniable Houthi action (Iran-aligned signal without attribution), (ii) Houthi action awaiting domestic framing, (iii) unrelated incident. This represents a potential break of the cycle's most stable non-event (80+ days of zero Red Sea attacks during Iran's multi-axis kinetic restart). **If a second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses; if not, this remains 'single incident, unclaimed.'** Until then: pattern undeclared, sample size 1. UN Security Council Resolution 2812 (2026) framework (monthly reporting through July 15) remains structurally intact pending attribution. Prior coordination-decay framework spanned Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, Project Freedom + pause cycle, May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, May 8 US tanker strikes, May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach. Persistent VLCC/Frontline rate elevation remains HORMUZ-driven, NOT Bab el-Mandeb-driven.

Confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days of May 19 'maritime blockade' announcement (by 2026-06-02)

Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement is leading indicator. Confirmed kinetic = action-tag upgrade; silence Day 14 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Mediterranean reach from Yemen is operationally weaker than Red Sea reach — favors silence outcome.

Note the regional kinetic environment has intensified on the LEBANON front (Hezbollah 1,000+ drones / 700+ rockets over 8 days), but that is a separate axis — the Houthi/Red-Sea axis remains rhetoric-without-action.

The companion Lloyd's-JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa trigger EXPIRED UNTRIPPED May 26 (no JWC listing). Analysts note the Houthis lack the naval reach to enforce a Mediterranean blockade and can only pressure shipowners who call at Haifa. The ~48h kinetic escalation is on the IRAN axis (US strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site) and the LEBANON front — neither a Houthi action.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-02: EXPIRED UNTRIPPED (deadline today, 2 June). Status moved `watching` -> `resolved`. The full 14-day window from the 19 May 'maritime blockade' announcement closed with NO confirmed qualifying Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping — a CONFIRMED non-event despite LOUD rhetoric this window (Iran/Tasnim vowed to 'activate the Bab al-Mandab,' the IRGC Quds Force commander threatened a 'traffic situation' in both Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz). Circulating 'confirmed attack' items trace to EARLIER dates (the July Magic Seas incident is later/separate; the 'Houthis announce end of attacks' headline is the May 2025 Oman-mediated pause — a stale-source trap, avoided). No edge weight change — rhetoric, not action. The 19 May verbal blockade is now externally falsified on both the kinetic limb (this trigger) and the insurance limb (Lloyd's JWC trigger, resolved 26 May).

active_triggers.condition: Lloyds JWC adds Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to high-risk listing within 7 days (by 2026-05-26)
active_triggers.note: Operational test of how the shipping community is reading the Saree-Haifa announcement — if Lloyds raises EastMed AWRP, the credibility weight of Houthi reach to Mediterranean is being priced; if Lloyds does not move, the 'rhetoric-only' tag is confirmed by the insurance market.
active_triggers.note_2026-05-25: Day 7 of 7. JWLA-033 (March 3) still operative with NO Eastern Med / Haifa addition through May 25. Window expires TOMORROW (May 26), on track to close UNTRIPPED. Kept at `watching` — NOT marked tripped, since it expires tomorrow not today.
active_triggers.note_2026-05-27: EXPIRED UNTRIPPED May 26 — no JWC listing. The 7-day window closed with Listed Areas still at JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) and no Eastern Mediterranean / Haifa addition. Status moved `watching` → `resolved` (expired untripped). The insurance market never priced the Haifa-blockade threat — the cleanest external falsification of the May 19 rhetoric. CONFIRMED non-event: no edge weight changes.

recent_signals.headline: **RHETORIC ESCALATED (BAB EL-MANDEB NOW EXPLICITLY IN THE THREAT SET) BUT ACTION STILL ABSENT — HAIFA-SHIPPING TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED TODAY.** Iran/Tasnim vowed the 'resistance front' would 'activate the Bab al-Mandab,' and the IRGC Quds Force commander threatened a 'traffic situation' in both Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz (CONFIRMED the threat was made; closure unimplemented). No confirmed new Houthi/Axis attack on neutral commercial shipping surfaced this window — coverage stays forecast/threat framing; circulating 'confirmed attack' items trace to earlier dates (July Magic Seas is later/separate; 'Houthis end attacks' is the May 2025 Oman pause — stale-source trap, avoided). The 'Confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping (by 2026-06-02)' trigger reaches its deadline today with NO qualifying attack — resolved EXPIRED UNTRIPPED, a confirmed non-event. No weight change.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' FALSIFIED — LLOYD'S JWC EASTERN-MED/HAIFA 7-DAY TRIGGER EXPIRED UNTRIPPED MAY 26 (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT); KINETIC-ATTACK TRIGGER STILL `watching` ~DAY 8 OF 14, UNTRIPPED.** The May 19 'long-distance naval blockade' of Haifa reached its first trigger deadline — Lloyd's Joint War Committee adds Eastern Mediterranean / Haifa within 7 days, by May 26 — with NO confirmed JWC listing (Listed Areas remain at JWLA-033, March 3, 2026) and NO confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel. The Lloyd's-JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa trigger therefore EXPIRED UNTRIPPED on May 26 — moved from `watching` → resolved (expired untripped). Analysts (Maritime Executive) note the Houthis lack the naval reach to enforce a Mediterranean blockade directly and can only pressure shipowners who choose to call at Haifa — not interdict vessels in the Eastern Med. This is the cleanest external falsification of the Haifa-blockade rhetoric: the insurance market never priced the threat. The separate Houthi-kinetic-on-Haifa-bound-shipping trigger remains `watching` (CONFIRMED non-event through May 27, ~Day 8 of 14, by 2026-06-02). The ~48h window's dominant kinetic story is US self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site (Iran axis) and the expanded Israeli Lebanon ground operation — NEITHER is a Houthi/Red-Sea action; the Houthi axis stays rhetoric-without-action. **A CONFIRMED non-event — no edge weight changes.**
sources: Maritime Executive — Houthi 'remote blockade' lacks naval reach for a Mediterranean enforcement, can only pressure shipowners calling at Haifa
Lloyd's List / Lloyd's Market Association — JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) unchanged through May 26, no Eastern Med / Haifa addition
ACLED — independent corroboration of no kinetic Haifa-bound vessel attack
recent_signals.verification: Lloyd's JWC Eastern-Med/Haifa listing change = CONFIRMED ABSENCE through the May 26 deadline (Lloyd's List / LMA primary; JWLA-033 still operative) — trigger EXPIRED UNTRIPPED, moved watching → resolved; Houthi kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel = CONFIRMED ABSENCE / non-event ~Day 8 of 14 (Maritime Executive, ACLED independent); analyst reach-assessment (Houthis cannot enforce a Mediterranean blockade, only pressure shipowners) = INFERRED structural reading corroborated by Maritime Executive. CONFIRMED non-event — no edge weight changes.

The Haifa-kinetic-attack trigger remains `watching` Day 7 of 14. The day's regional escalation is on the LEBANON front (Hezbollah drone/rocket campaign, US greenlight weighed) — a separate axis; the Houthi/Red-Sea axis stays rhetoric-without-action.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS, NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK; LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after May 19 announcement, no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to Persian Gulf coverage — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7** (by 2026-05-26); confirmed kinetic attack trigger `watching` Day 4 of 14. **Israeli media confirms negative space**: Times of Israel May 22 liveblog + Haaretz carry no Haifa-attack incident reporting May 20-22; insurance market AWRP ~1% holding from May 19. **Atlantic Council framing validated to Day 4**: 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets' — rhetoric WITHOUT operational follow-through is the dominant pattern. **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. The simultaneity with the IRGC May 21 'global offensive' threat (CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION) reinforces the pattern: rhetorical escalation along multiple axes, but the operational floor holds.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF ISRAEL'S HAIFA PORT — CONFIRMED RHETORIC PIVOT, NO OPERATIONAL FOLLOW-THROUGH; REVERSES MONDAY'S 'COORDINATED QUIETISM' READING.** Spokesman Yahya Saree said in a televised statement: 'Haifa has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets' and warned all companies with ships present in or heading to Haifa that the port had been 'included in the list of targets.' The Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added that a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply, with all vessels 'prohibited' from loading or unloading at Israel's largest seaport. The decision was framed as response to Israeli military operations in Gaza, **NOT** as response to US-Iran kinetic preparations — a subtle but consequential framing choice. **The announcement IS calibrated**: (i) targets Israel directly, not US/UK shipping; (ii) Haifa is on the Israeli MEDITERRANEAN coast, not Red Sea — 'long-distance naval blockade' of a Mediterranean port from Yemen is more rhetorical than operational; (iii) the announcement is justified by Gaza, preserving Houthi option to claim they haven't entered the Iran-US theater. **Maritime Executive headline uses scare quotes around 'blockade' precisely because it has NOT been operationalized**: no attack on a vessel attempting to dock at Haifa has been independently confirmed. Saree cited the Eilat-port blockade as precedent and claimed Eilat 'has since ceased operations' (REPORTED Houthi claim; independent maritime confirmation pending — Eilat is Red Sea-adjacent, structurally different from Haifa's Mediterranean position). Atlantic Council / Stimson analytical framing: 'targeted provocations highlight the fundamental problem with partial regional ceasefires — they merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' **The 'coordinated quietism' framing was a graduated-rhetoric pattern, NOT a structural restraint** — Iran's proxy network can pivot from non-attack (last 7 days) to verbal-blockade within 48 hours. **NEW trigger ADDED at 'watching'**: confirmed missile/drone aimed at Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days = action-tag upgrade to CONFIRMED ACTION; silence through Day 14 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Israel-government and IDF responses to the announcement not surfaced through Tuesday morning IST. Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be cleanest operational test — if Lloyds elevates the EastMed war-risk premium, the 'rhetoric-only' tag weakens. **The announcement lands on the same day as Trump's postponement post — re-armed proxy rhetoric overlay on the postponed kinetic axis is a Sadjadpour 'two hubris' second-order signal: Iran's proxy network reasserts deniable optionality precisely when the diplomatic axis pauses.**
sources: Maritime Executive 'Houthis Reboot Red Sea Crisis With 'Blockade' on Port of Haifa'
Atlantic Council / Stimson Center analytical framing
recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa announcement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Saree statement multi-source; the announcement is a verified public statement). Substantive claim ('blockade operational') = RHETORIC tag (NOT operationalized through confirmed attacks). Houthi Eilat-cessation claim = REPORTED (single-source Saree; independent maritime confirmation of Eilat operational status pending). Mediterranean reach from Yemen = operational reality check (structurally weaker than Red Sea reach).

recent_signals.headline: **A4 RED SEA DAY 7 OF 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW CLOSES UNTRIPPED — May 11 Hodeidah reverts to 'single unclaimed event'** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO recent-incidents page primary, MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement). The trigger 'Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory' on strait-of-hormuz node moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern**: NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu phone call May 17, Israeli security cabinet May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17. The houthis→red-sea and red-sea→strait-of-hormuz edges carry forward at high weight but the *absence* of action under a multi-vector US escalation cycle is operationally consistent with Houthi command being instructed by Iran to hold fire. **Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts and Mojtaba Day 11 of state-media silence** — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. The investor read: Iran is signalling escalation control, not escalation, against Washington's posturing. The non-action read is structurally validated through one full negative-space cycle.
sources: UKMTO recent-incidents page (operational primary, no new advisory through Monday IST)
Gard Insights 'Red Sea update: Resumption of Houthi campaign' (industry assessment)

If no second incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than pattern start. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak (JPost + CNN intel) = Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — HOUTHI RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel briefly 'not under command' but continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, breaking the 80-day quiet that had spanned Iran's multi-axis restart + Project Freedom cycles + Hezbollah cross-border reach. The non-claim is itself a signal: deniable Houthi action, action awaiting framing, or unrelated incident. **Trigger window: if second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1, pattern undeclared. Coordination-decay framework not yet falsified but under first material test in 80 days.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET — NO HOUTHI ATTACKS ON COMMERCIAL SHIPPING IN PAST 24 HOURS; UNSCR 2812 MONTHLY REPORTING FRAME INTACT (NEXT REPORT JULY 15). UKMTO advisories continue to flag heightened threat level but no incidents. Houthis reiterated rhetorical solidarity with Iran across early-cycle messaging but operational silence is now in 80th consecutive day. Houthi non-response now spans (a) Iran's May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart, (b) US Project Freedom + pause cycle, (c) the May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, (d) the May 7-8 second day of US tanker strikes, (e) the May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach (corrected to adjacent territory), AND now (f) the Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike. The full proxy-restraint pattern is multi-event confirmed: even when other Iranian proxies (Hezbollah) demonstrate first cross-border reach AND when an unclaimed strike occurs near a fellow GCC mediator's coast, Houthis remain quiet. The non-action remains the signal.
